attacking_style: Sprint finisher who positions on the left side of the road in contested bunch finishes. Not a primary attacker but opportunistic in final meters.
team_tactics: Insufficient data to determine clear team setup patterns. Appears to contest sprints independently when opportunity arises.
terrain_preference: Flat to rolling stages with bunch sprint finishes. Benefits from reduced pure sprinter fields in stage race contexts.
vulnerability: Susceptible to illness during multi-day racing events, resulting in significant performance drops on consecutive stages.
key_patterns: Wins bunch sprints in stage races through positioning and persistence rather than pure speed.
Shows mental resilience to recover from poor form days within same stage race.
Takes advantage of disqualifications and chaos in sprint finishes to claim victories.
form_trajectory: positive: demonstrated ability to recover quickly from illness-induced poor performance within same race week
